UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores additional profile information for users authenticated via Firebase Authentication. This entity holds data beyond what Firebase Auth natively manages, such as custom display names or application-specific settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, which corresponds to the Firebase Authentication user ID.

email.description: The user's primary email address, obtained from Firebase Authentication.

displayName.description: The user's display name, typically provided by their authentication provider (e.g., Google) or a custom value set by the user.

photoUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, usually provided by their authentication provider if available.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's profile was first created in the application.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the user's most recent login activity.

ToolUsageLog.title: ToolUsageLog
ToolUsageLog.description: Records each instance of a user utilizing an AI or utility tool, including the inputs provided and the results generated. This forms the user's history.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific tool usage log entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity, indicating which user performed this action. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ToolUsageLog)

toolName.description: The descriptive name of the AI or utility tool that was used (e.g., 'Instagram Bio Generator', 'QR Code Generator').

timestamp.description: The date and time when the tool was used.

inputData.description: The input data or parameters provided by the user to the tool, stored as a serialized string (e.g., JSON string).

outputResult.description: The result or output generated by the tool based on the provided input, stored as a serialized string (e.g., JSON string or direct text output).

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. Each document's ID is the Firebase Authentication UID of the user. Access is restricted to the owner (the user whose UID matches the document ID).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID.

definition.description: Stores a historical record of each tool usage by a specific user. This is a subcollection under a user's profile. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path itself for authorization independence and QAPs. Access is restricted to the owner (the user whose UID matches the parent 'userId').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID of the owner of this log entry.

params.name: toolUsageLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific tool usage log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the 'Ultra All AI' platform prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability in line with the core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Authorization for user-specific data is intrinsically tied to the path structure, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For user profiles, the path `/users/{userId}` means the document ID itself is the `userId`, allowing rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard without fetching any parent document. Similarly, for `ToolUsageLog` entries at `/users/{userId}/toolUsageLogs/{toolUsageLogId}`, the `userId` in the path is denormalized directly from the parent user. The `ToolUsageLog` entity also explicitly includes a `userId` field, further reinforcing this direct ownership link. This ensures that creating, updating, or deleting these documents does not incur additional reads for authorization checks, maintaining atomicity and performance.

**Support for Query-Aware Permissions (QAPs):**
The structural segregation ensures that `list` operations are secure by design. Each user's data (profile and tool usage logs) resides in a subcollection directly under their unique user ID (`/users/{userId}`). This path-based ownership means that any `list` query initiated by a client *must* include `request.auth.uid` in the path to retrieve their own data. For example, a query for `toolUsageLogs` can only be performed securely if it targets `/users/request.auth.uid/toolUsageLogs`. Firestore security rules can then easily enforce `allow read: if request.auth.uid == userId;`, guaranteeing that users can only read their own data and preventing unauthorized data leakage through broad `list` operations. This structure naturally enforces QAPs without complex rule logic or filters, as the rules act as validators for the client's requested path.
